rationales.hedonism: She spent 'the rest of the afternoon annoyed at myself' over 'ten minutes of sitting with cheese,' treating a small pleasure as something to regret rather than enjoy.
rationales.achievement: The entry centers on work output, noting she'd 'already put in' six hours and feeling she should have gone 'straight back to the loom.'

rationales.hedonism: After finishing the panel, she 'let myself leave the studio at four,' bought a warm 'cardamom bun,' and enjoyed the walk home without guilt.
rationales.achievement: She 'finished the second panel for the Kungsbacka commission' and took real satisfaction in how 'the indigo section catches the light.'

rationales.hedonism: She and Karin 'brought wine,' made 'a ridiculous amount of pasta,' and spent three hours enjoying themselves enough that she 'didn't think about the commission once.'

rationales.self_direction: She says 'I had opinions but didn't push them' and keeps her own work talk short, holding back her views instead of acting on them.
rationales.conformity: She stayed quiet because she's 'only there two days a week' and felt it was 'not my place,' showing restraint to fit the group's expectations.

rationales.self_direction: She went in 'by seven' because she 'wanted to see how it would fall across the third panel,' following her own artistic judgment rather than a fixed schedule.
rationales.achievement: She worked from early morning until one in a focused stretch where 'the work was pulling me along' and the technical setup was 'exactly right.'

rationales.hedonism: Even with the pull to 'get another hour in,' she chose sleep instead, saying stopping felt right because 'the day had already been full.'

rationales.self_direction: He says the building feels more like his after everyone leaves, with 'No one asking me to fill out forms or attend alignment meetings,' showing how much he values working on his own terms.
rationales.achievement: He 'stayed late grading because I refuse to bring that stack home again,' pushing through the workload to get it done.

rationales.self_direction: He challenged the district plan by asking 'what happens to the electives I built' and bristled at a packaged curriculum replacing work he designed himself.
rationales.conformity: While 'Everyone just nodded,' he raised his hand and objected, refusing to go along with the room's passive acceptance.

rationales.self_direction: He rejects the 'shrink-wrapped curriculum,' insisting good teaching comes from 'giving kids room to chase something that matters to them and getting out of the way.'
rationales.benevolence: He told Daniela her project was 'one of the best I have seen in nearly three decades,' giving her personal encouragement when she looked 'genuinely shocked.'

rationales.self_direction: What troubles him is that taking the role would mean helping dismantle something he created 'on my own terms,' and that feels 'backwards' to him.
rationales.power: He recoils from accepting it for 'a title and a small say,' treating institutional status as a compromise rather than something worth chasing.

rationales.self_direction: He 'Told Reynolds no' and spent the weekend making things on his own terms, from the cutting board pattern to the way he chose to spend his time.
rationales.hedonism: The entry lingers on chosen pleasures like the farmers market, slow woodworking where 'Nobody was timing me,' and a house that felt 'Quiet house, but not an empty one.'
rationales.benevolence: He built a cutting board 'for my neighbor's birthday' and wrote Daniela 'a recommendation letter she did not ask for,' going out of his way for people close to him.

rationales.self_direction: She told Farhan, 'I'm deciding the menu, not his mother,' asserting her own authority over the dinner plans.
rationales.achievement: She spent the afternoon 'editing two product review posts' and took satisfaction that the comments were 'real questions' from mothers seeking her opinion.
rationales.power: Both insisting on the menu and feeling good that other mothers were 'asking my opinion on the brand' show her satisfaction in having influence.

rationales.self_direction: She wanted to say 'this is my daughter's room' but instead said 'okay, thank you,' leaving her own choices unasserted.
rationales.power: The whole episode turns on Ammi 'rearrang[ing] it without even asking me,' making clear Nadia has little control even over her daughter's room.
rationales.conformity: She swallows the objection because 'what happens if I push back?' and describes the household 'pecking order,' choosing peace over open conflict.

rationales.self_direction: She can't act on something important because 'I can't just do it myself' and has to wait on someone else to press the button.
rationales.power: What tightens her jaw is that Zara's appointment 'depends on someone else remembering' while she can only 'sit and hope they get around to it.'

rationales.self_direction: She says yes immediately to the collab and stresses 'Nobody needs to hand me a script,' framing the work as something she directs herself.
rationales.achievement: She spent the afternoon 'prepping talking points, researching the specs, writing out comparison notes,' showing pride in mastering the material.
rationales.power: The collaboration request and Farhan saying her page is 'doing well' both signal that her voice is gaining reach and influence.

rationales.self_direction: She had 'planned the whole menu,' but Ammi 'just started making her own daal' and overrode the plan without asking.
rationales.power: What she cannot let go of is that Ammi walked in 'like the kitchen was hers,' while Nadia's hours of work were treated as interchangeable.
rationales.conformity: Even after being overridden, she 'smiled' through dinner and let the evening proceed instead of openly challenging the situation.

rationales.self_direction: She had done the school research, yet when Abba spoke 'he wasn't even talking to me,' leaving her unable to direct a decision she had prepared for.
rationales.power: The sting is that she 'didn't register as someone whose input counted,' especially in a discussion about her own daughter's school.
rationales.conformity: She says nothing in the moment, goes to the bedroom afterward, and keeps replaying it rather than confronting the men at the table.

rationales.self_direction: She contrasts the house with the internet and notes that online 'people actually listen,' highlighting a space where her choices stick.
rationales.achievement: Getting a free product, seeing 'Two hundred views in the first hour,' and screenshotting the analytics show clear satisfaction in measurable performance.
rationales.power: She explicitly notices that 'strangers treat my opinion like it matters' and that her recommendation carries weight with people she doesn't know.

rationales.self_direction: She emphasizes 'every choice was mine' and calls it 'the first time in a long time I feel like I'm steering something,' without asking permission.
rationales.achievement: The 'feedback loop is immediate and clear' and the growing view count make the work feel rewarding because she can see the results of what she made.
rationales.power: Being validated by 'a parenting micro-influencer with eight thousand followers' and watched by 'three hundred strangers' gives her a strong sense of influence.

rationales.achievement: She says 'If you put in the work to learn something properly, people should listen,' framing others' trust as something earned through competence.
rationales.power: What feels new is 'being the person other people check with before spending their money,' which gives her clear influence over their choices.

rationales.self_direction: The pattern she describes is that decisions move around her and 'I find out later,' showing she has little say in matters that concern her.
rationales.power: She is shaken because Farhan told his mother first about 'our daughter's school,' underscoring how little authority she has in the household.
rationales.conformity: At dinner she 'just kept eating' and 'didn't even look up,' containing her reaction instead of challenging the slight in the moment.
